title: AI in Education: Teacher Readiness and AI-TPACK Survey Dataset with Qualitative Coding (India, N = 208)
author: Kulkarni, Heramb
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset contains anonymised quantitative and qualitative data from a mixed-method research study on Artificial Intelligence (AI) integration in education, focusing on teacher readiness, AI-TPACK competencies, and systemic challenges. 
 The dataset includes responses from 208 educators across India (Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Rajasthan, and other regions). 
 FILES INCLUDED: 
 1. Anonymised_Data.xlsx - Survey_Data: 208 participants × 28 variables - Likert-scale responses converted to numeric (0–4) - Domain scores: CK, PK, AI-TK, AI-TCK, AI-TPK, AI-TPACK - Participant_ID anonymisation (T001–T208) - No personally identifiable information (PII) 
 - Qualitative_Responses: Verbatim responses to 5 open-ended questions linked via Participant_ID 
 2. Coded_Dataset.xlsx - Codebook: 10 thematic codes (deductive + inductive) - Coded_Qualitative_Data: All responses coded - Theme_Frequency: Distribution of themes (κ = 0.81) - AI_TPACK_Scores: Individual and aggregate scores - Confidence_Scores: AI confidence classification - README: Ethics, anonymisation protocol, and coding methodology 
 RESEARCH CONTEXT: This dataset supports analysis of teacher readiness, AI adoption, pedagogical shifts, ethical concerns, and policy gaps in AI-enabled education. 
 All data have been anonymised in compliance with GDPR and research ethics standards. 
 This dataset is suitable for secondary research, AI in education studies, and policy analysis.
